frameworks.name: optimal decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: confidence as readout of probability that a decision is correct

frameworks.name: subjective confidence
frameworks.one_line: confidence inferred from observers' behavioral reports

frameworks.name: signal detection / perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: sensorimotor circuits jointly support choice and confidence

frameworks.name: population decoding
frameworks.one_line: multivariate decoder predicts choice from neuronal ensemble activity

core_question: Does superior colliculus ensemble activity reflect optimal confidence or subjective confidence?
key_assumptions: decision accuracy and reported confidence can in principle be dissociated
optimal confidence tracks the probability of being correct
ensemble decoding indexes the decision-relevant signal in SC
manipulations: two motion discrimination tasks: one where accuracy and confidence co-vary, one where they are dissociated
stimuli matched for decision accuracy but producing different subjective confidence
key_findings: decoding performance from SC ensembles increased with decision accuracy
when accuracy was matched, decoding was similar across high vs low subjective confidence
interpretations: SC signals optimal decision confidence, similar to cortical mechanisms
SC is unlikely to play a critical role in subjective confidence
